title: Functions should use return consistently
description: Functions should have consistent return behavior across all execution paths. A function should either always return values or never return values (void function). Mixed return patterns where some paths return values while others return nothing (undefined) can lead to unexpected behavior and make the function's contract unclear to callers.
recommendation: Ensure all execution paths in a function return the same type. Either make all paths return values, or make all paths return nothing (void).
impact: Code clarity and potential runtime issues

noncompliant: function inconsistent(x) { if (x > 0) { return x; } // Missing return for x <= 0 }
function mixed(flag) { if (flag) { return 'value'; } else { return; } }
function incomplete(a, b) { if (a > b) { return a; } else if (b > a) { return b; } // Missing return for a === b }
compliant: function consistent(x) { if (x > 0) { return x; } else { return 0; } }
function voidFunction(x) { if (x > 0) { console.log(x); } // No returns anywhere }
function complete(a, b) { if (a > b) { return a; } else { return b; } }
